question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you export a puppet file to the desktop?
answer: Click the 'File' menu in the top-left corner, select 'Export', choose 'Puppet' in the dialog box, navigate to the desktop folder, enter '2D Character for Character Animator.puppet' in the 'File name' field, and click 'Save'.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'File' menu item located in the top-left corner of the application window.
From the 'File' dropdown menu, the 'Export' option is selected by a mouse click.
In the export dialog box, the user selects the 'Puppet' option from the list.
The user navigates to the desktop folder using the file path navigation pane on the left side of the export dialog box.
The user types '2D Character for Character Animator.puppet' into the 'File name' text field within the export dialog box.
The user clicks the 'Save' button at the bottom-right corner of the export dialog box.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you import a puppet into a new project and add it to the scene?
answer: Create a new project via 'File' > 'New Project', import the puppet using 'File' > 'Import', select the '2D Character for Character Animator.puppet' file from the desktop, then drag the puppet from the project panel to the scene area.
keypoints: A new project is created by selecting 'New Project' from the 'File' menu.
The user imports the puppet file by clicking on the 'Import' option under the 'File' menu.
The user navigates to the desktop folder again and selects the '2D Character for Character Animator.puppet' file.
The imported puppet appears in the project panel on the left side of the screen.
The user drags the puppet from the project panel onto the scene area in the center of the screen.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you configure a character to walk using arrow keys?
answer: Click the 'Triggers' section in the right-side panel, then change the walk mode to 'Left & Right Arrow keys' using the dropdown menu in the 'Walk' settings.
keypoints: The user adjusts the walk settings by clicking on the 'Triggers' section in the right-side panel.
The user changes the walk mode to 'Left & Right Arrow keys' using the dropdown menu within the 'Walk' settings.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, where is the 'Triggers' section located for adjusting walk settings?
answer: The 'Triggers' section is located in the right-side panel of the interface.
keypoints: The user adjusts the walk settings by clicking on the 'Triggers' section in the right-side panel.
